summary: ACCEPT. Independent read-only functional QA review finds the repaired R2 package smoke sufficient for M5-WEB-WINDOWS-DISTRIBUTABLE-001 at the current M5 slice boundary. The packaged Electron smoke launches the M5 playable workspace from the unsigned package, exercises start, preview, confirm, client-session save, cancel, and load recovery, and the required Web build, security check, package build, Chromium e2e, Electron host-hash canary, handoff validation, task validation, desktop checks, and diff checks pass. Import/export button count 0 is accepted as an explicit functional caveat because the current M5 client surface implements client-session save/load checkpoint behavior, the slice definition allows load-save checkpoint as the minimum save boundary, and the current desktop preload/IPC surface intentionally has no file import/export bridge.

tests_run.result: Clean working tree on chore/m5-web-windows-distributable before QA handoff creation.
Changed paths are apps/desktop/scripts/package-smoke.mjs, apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts, and allowed project task/message/state files only.

Reviewed exact product-code diff. main.ts only adds MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB packaged web entry selection while preserving MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY priority; package-smoke launches packaged Electron and verifies M5 workspace, runtime bridge, command preview/confirm, save, cancel, and load.
Vite production Web build passed; dist/index.html and dist/host-hash-canary.html generated. Existing chunk-size warning is non-blocking.

Electron security check passed. nodeIntegration=false, contextIsolation=true, sandbox=true, renderer file navigation restricted, preload exposes only monsoonDesktop, and allowed IPC channels are desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Unsigned local package generated at .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64 with main, preload, renderer, web assets, and platform adapter artifacts.

PASS. Packaged app launched M5 workspace, runtime bridge was present, command preview/confirm passed, client-session save wrote m5.save.client-session-written, cancel/load restored m5.load.client-session-restored, final phase was running, confirmed count was 1, and Import/export button count was 0 as caveat.
PASS. Electron renderer dedicated worker matched Node baseline: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.
PASS. 8 Chromium tests passed, including M5 playable flow through save/load, success/failure, and required desktop viewport overflow checks.
PASS. All desktop matched files use Prettier code style.
PASS. Desktop ESLint completed without findings.
PASS. Desktop TypeScript noEmit check completed.

tests_run.result: No scope-drift hits in changed product files; the only match was the literal test label string 'M5 load status'.

decisions: ACCEPT route_to=lead_orchestrator. This QA handoff does not mark the task CLOSED.

The repaired package smoke satisfies the M5-WEB-WINDOWS-DISTRIBUTABLE-001 acceptance criteria at the current M5 slice boundary: it proves local Web build output, unsigned packaged Electron launch, M5 playable workspace availability, command flow, client-session save/load recovery, security canary, and deterministic Electron host hash canary.

Import/export button count 0 is acceptable as an explicit caveat, not a REQUEST_CHANGES blocker. The current M5 UI exposes Save checkpoint and Load checkpoint, packages/client-core provides a schema-checked M5 client-session save parser, M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 allows save export/import or load-save checkpoint at minimum, and the desktop preload/IPC surface intentionally exposes no file import/export bridge.

The caveat means no desktop file import/export bridge or OS file picker behavior is accepted by this review. Any future desktop file import/export must be a separate task with allowlisted dialogs, schema validation, path constraints, malformed-input tests, and security review.

The required test representation is sufficient. The task wording lists pnpm e2e, but this repository's actual script is pnpm test:e2e; R2 recorded the alias caveat and QA reran pnpm test:e2e successfully.

No scope drift found into telemetry, accounts, network services, store integration, signing, branding, commercial/public release, release manifests, CI, content-source, sim-core, sim-ai, tools, or gate/roadmap/ADR files.

risks.risk: The current ACCEPT covers client-session save/load checkpoint behavior only. It does not prove file import/export UX or desktop file-system adapter behavior because those surfaces do not exist in this slice.

MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB is an evidence switch for packaged smoke; normal desktop mode still loads the security shell. A future product decision to make the packaged app open the M5 web UI by default would need an explicit task and review.
package-smoke intentionally fails rather than bypasses if MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY is forced in the caller environment because main.ts preserves host-hash priority.

requested_action: Lead may integrate this ACCEPT QA review with the existing security ACCEPT and decide the next process step. Do not treat this handoff as CLOSED; it is functional QA acceptance with the documented import/export caveat.
